evals.name: springboot-community-java-maven-default
evals.prompt: How do I start with Flamingock in my Spring Boot app?
evals.expected_output: A complete Maven + Spring Boot Community setup using Java, with canonical placeholders such as [VERSION], TODO, and technology-specific hand-off guidance.
expectations: Uses [VERSION] instead of legacy version placeholders

evals.name: standalone-cloud-kotlin-gradle
evals.prompt: I want to use Flamingock Cloud in a standalone Kotlin project with Gradle.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin snippet using Flamingock.builder() with Cloud apiToken/environment/service configuration, TODO placeholders, and Gradle plugin setup.
expectations: Uses the canonical Cloud wording: apiToken, environment, and service
Uses TODO placeholders for unresolved Cloud values
Configures Gradle plugin without community() module

evals.name: onboarding-hand-off-to-targetsystem
evals.prompt: Flamingock is already integrated in my app. Now help me connect MongoDB.
evals.expected_output: Either a clear redirect to the MongoDB targetsystem skill or a helpful MongoDB targetsystem next-step response that respects the existing Flamingock setup, without redoing onboarding.
expectations: Recognizes Flamingock is already set up
Either redirects to flamingock-mongodb-sync-targetsystem or gives targetsystem-focused next steps that fit the current context

evals.name: onboarding-hand-off-to-change-skill
evals.prompt: My TargetSystem is already configured. I need a migration class for MongoDB now.
evals.expected_output: Either a hand-off to the MongoDB change skill or a helpful migration-oriented response that respects the already configured TargetSystem, without generating onboarding output.
expectations: Recognizes the TargetSystem is already configured
Either redirects to flamingock-mongodb-sync-change or gives migration-oriented help that fits the current context
